definitions.text: One of two usually roughly equal parts into which anything may be divided, or considered as divided.
examples: I ate the smaller half of the apple.
You don’t know the half of it.

definitions.text: One of two equal periods into which a game is divided.
examples: An association football match usually consists of two halves of 45 minutes each.

definitions.text: One of the two opposite parts of the playing field of various sports, in which each starts the game.
Consisting of some indefinite portion resembling a half; approximately a half, whether more or less; partial; imperfect.
Having one parent (rather than two) in common.
examples: A half brother or half sister

definitions.text: In two equal parts or to an equal degree.
In some part approximating a half.

examples: The task is only half done.

definitions.text: Half past; a half-hour (30 minutes) after the last hour.
examples: The time is 9:30; it is half nine.

definitions.text: (Discuss this sense) A half-hour to (preceding) the next hour.
A call reminding performers that the performance will begin in thirty minutes.
